examples.grammarPoint: A につけ B につけ
examples.sentence: 彼には笑うにつけ泣くにつけ、とても魅力的だ。
examples.translation: He is very charming whether he’s laughing or crying.
examples.translationEn: He is very charming whether he’s laughing or crying.
examples.romaji: Kare ni wa warau ni tsuke naku ni tsuke, totemo miryokuteki da.

examples.grammarPoint: A につけ B につけ
examples.sentence: 嬉しいにつけ悲しいにつけ、彼はいつも音楽を聴いている。
examples.translation: Whether he's happy or sad, he's always listening to music.
examples.translationEn: Whether he's happy or sad, he's always listening to music.
examples.romaji: Ureshii ni tsuke kanashii ni tsuke, kare wa itsumo ongaku o kiite iru.

examples.grammarPoint: A につけ B につけ
examples.sentence: 日中につけ夜間につけ、この景色は美しい。
examples.translation: This view is beautiful whether it's daytime or nighttime.
examples.translationEn: This view is beautiful whether it's daytime or nighttime.
examples.romaji: Nitchuu ni tsuke yakan ni tsuke, kono keishiki wa utsukushii.

examples.grammarPoint: A につけ B につけ
examples.sentence: 喜びにつけ悲しみにつけ、彼女はいつも私を支えてくれる。
examples.translation: Whether I’m joyful or sorrowful, she always supports me.
examples.translationEn: Whether I’m joyful or sorrowful, she always supports me.
examples.romaji: Yorokobi ni tsuke kanashimi ni tsuke, kanojo wa itsumo watashi o sasaete kureru.

examples.grammarPoint: A のやら B のやら
examples.sentence: 彼が怒っているのやら、悲しいのやら、私には分からない。
examples.translation: I don't know whether he's angry or sad.
examples.translationEn: I don't know whether he's angry or sad.
examples.romaji: Kare ga okotte iru no yara, kanashii no yara, watashi ni wa wakaranai.

examples.grammarPoint: A のやら B のやら
examples.sentence: 試験の結果が良いのやら悪いのやら、気になって仕方がない。
examples.translation: I can’t help worrying whether my exam results are good or bad.
examples.translationEn: I can’t help worrying whether my exam results are good or bad.
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka ga yoi no yara warui no yara, ki ni natte shikata ga nai.

examples.grammarPoint: A のやら B のやら
examples.sentence: 彼女が私を好きなのやら嫌いなのやら、わからない。
examples.translation: I don’t know whether she likes me or hates me.
examples.translationEn: I don’t know whether she likes me or hates me.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga watashi o suki na no yara kirai na no yara, wakaranai.

examples.grammarPoint: A のやら B のやら
examples.sentence: 彼が本当に行くのやら行かないのやら、はっきりしない。
examples.translation: It’s unclear whether he will really go or not.
examples.translationEn: It’s unclear whether he will really go or not.
examples.romaji: Kare ga hontou ni iku no yara ikanai no yara, hakkiri shinai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 鬼が鬼なら、人間も人間だ。
examples.translation: If demons are indeed demons, humans are likewise humans.
examples.translationEn: If demons are indeed demons, humans are likewise humans.
examples.romaji: Oni ga oni nara, ningen mo ningen da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 彼が社長なら、私も社長だ。
examples.translation: If he is the president, then I'm the president too.
examples.translationEn: If he is the president, then I'm the president too.
examples.romaji: Kare ga shachou nara, watashi mo shachou da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 貴様が戦士なら、俺も戦士だ。
examples.translation: If you're a warrior, then I'm a warrior as well.
examples.translationEn: If you're a warrior, then I'm a warrior as well.
examples.romaji: Kisama ga senshi nara, ore mo senshi da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 が Noun1 なら、 Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: ハムスターがペットなら、猫もペットだ。
examples.translation: If a hamster counts as a pet, then a cat is also a pet.
examples.translationEn: If a hamster counts as a pet, then a cat is also a pet.
examples.romaji: Hamusutā ga petto nara, neko mo petto da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 君も君なら、私も私だ。
examples.translation: If you are you, then I am also me.
examples.translationEn: If you are you, then I am also me.
examples.romaji: Kimi mo kimi nara, watashi mo watashi da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 夏も夏なら、冬も冬だ。
examples.translation: If it’s said that summer is summer, then winter is also winter.
examples.translationEn: If it’s said that summer is summer, then winter is also winter.
examples.romaji: Natsu mo natsu nara, fuyu mo fuyu da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 東京も東京なら、大阪も大阪だ。
examples.translation: If Tokyo is Tokyo, then Osaka is also Osaka (they each have their distinctive qualities).
examples.translationEn: If Tokyo is Tokyo, then Osaka is also Osaka (they each have their distinctive qualities).
examples.romaji: Toukyou mo toukyou nara, Oosaka mo Oosaka da.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun1 も Noun1 なら、Noun2 も Noun2 だ
examples.sentence: 犬も犬なら、猫も猫だ。
examples.translation: If dogs are dogs, then cats are also cats.
examples.translationEn: If dogs are dogs, then cats are also cats.
examples.romaji: Inu mo inu nara, neko mo neko da.

examples.sentence: 家族あっての私です。
examples.translation: I am who I am thanks to my family.
examples.translationEn: I am who I am thanks to my family.
examples.romaji: Kazoku atte no watashi desu.

examples.sentence: お客様あってのビジネスです。
examples.translation: A business is nothing without its customers.
examples.translationEn: A business is nothing without its customers.
examples.romaji: Okyakusama atte no bijinesu desu.

examples.sentence: 練習あっての成功です。
examples.translation: Success is nothing without practice.
examples.translationEn: Success is nothing without practice.
examples.romaji: Renshuu atte no seikou desu.

examples.sentence: 健康あっての仕事です。
examples.translation: Work is nothing without your health.
examples.translationEn: Work is nothing without your health.
examples.romaji: Kenkou atte no shigoto desu.

examples.sentence: 東京へ出張のかたがた、友人に会いに行った。
examples.translation: While on a business trip to Tokyo, I also went to see a friend.
examples.translationEn: While on a business trip to Tokyo, I also went to see a friend.
examples.romaji: Toukyou e shucchou no kata gata, yuujin ni ai ni itta.

examples.sentence: 観光のかたがた、お土産を買いました。
examples.translation: While sightseeing, I bought some souvenirs.
examples.translationEn: While sightseeing, I bought some souvenirs.
examples.romaji: Kankou no kata gata, omiyage o kaimashita.

examples.sentence: 散歩のかたがた、ゴミを拾った。
examples.translation: While taking a walk, I also picked up trash.
examples.translationEn: While taking a walk, I also picked up trash.
examples.romaji: Sanpo no kata gata, gomi o hirotta.

examples.sentence: 学習のかたがた、音楽を聞いた。
examples.translation: While studying, I also listened to music.
examples.translationEn: While studying, I also listened to music.
examples.romaji: Gakushuu no kata gata, ongaku o kiita.

examples.sentence: 散歩がてらに、近くの公園で花を摘みました。
examples.translation: While taking a walk, I picked some flowers in the nearby park.
examples.translationEn: While taking a walk, I picked some flowers in the nearby park.
examples.romaji: Sanpo gatera ni, chikaku no kouen de hana o tsumimashita.

examples.sentence: 買い物がてら、友達に会いに行きました。
examples.translation: While I was out shopping, I went to see a friend.
examples.translationEn: While I was out shopping, I went to see a friend.
examples.romaji: Kaimono gatera, tomodachi ni ai ni ikimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は旅行がてら、新しい言語を勉強しました。
examples.translation: She studied a new language while traveling.
examples.translationEn: She studied a new language while traveling.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa ryokou gatera, atarashii gengo o benkyou shimashita.

examples.sentence: ドライブがてら、新しいアルバムを聞きました。
examples.translation: I listened to a new album while going for a drive.
examples.translationEn: I listened to a new album while going for a drive.
examples.romaji: Doraibu gatera, atarashii arubamu o kikimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼のコレクションは500個からある。
examples.translation: He has at least 500 items in his collection.
examples.translationEn: He has at least 500 items in his collection.
examples.romaji: Kare no korekushon wa gohyaku ko kara aru.

examples.sentence: このビルは40階からある。
examples.translation: This building has at least 40 floors.
examples.translationEn: This building has at least 40 floors.
examples.romaji: Kono biru wa yonjuu kai kara aru.

examples.sentence: 彼の財産は10億円からある。
examples.translation: He has assets worth at least one billion yen.
examples.translationEn: He has assets worth at least one billion yen.
examples.romaji: Kare no zaisan wa juu oku en kara aru.

examples.sentence: このマラソンは10kmからある。
examples.translation: This marathon is at least 10 kilometers long.
examples.translationEn: This marathon is at least 10 kilometers long.
examples.romaji: Kono marason wa juu kiro kara aru.

examples.sentence: 彼の態度からすると、彼はその計画に反対しているようだ。
examples.translation: Judging from his attitude, he seems to be against the plan.
examples.translationEn: Judging from his attitude, he seems to be against the plan.
examples.romaji: Kare no taido kara suru to, kare wa sono keikaku ni hantai shiteiru you da.

examples.sentence: このレストランの雰囲気からすると、料理もすばらしいはずだ。
examples.translation: Judging from the atmosphere of this restaurant, the food must be wonderful.
examples.translationEn: Judging from the atmosphere of this restaurant, the food must be wonderful.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran no fun'iki kara suru to, ryouri mo subarashii hazu da.

examples.sentence: 彼女の成績からすると、彼女は非常に勉強熱心な人でしょう。
examples.translation: Judging from her grades, she must be a very diligent student.
examples.translationEn: Judging from her grades, she must be a very diligent student.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no seiseki kara suru to, kanojo wa hijou ni benkyou nesshin na hito deshou.

examples.sentence: その会社の成長率からすると、将来的には大きな利益を得られるでしょう。
examples.translation: From the company's growth rate, it seems likely to gain substantial profits in the future.
examples.translationEn: From the company's growth rate, it seems likely to gain substantial profits in the future.
examples.romaji: Sono kaisha no seicho-ritsu kara suru to, shouraiteki ni wa ooki na rieki o erareru deshou.

examples.sentence: 日本からの手紙を受け取りました。
examples.translation: I received a letter from Japan.
examples.translationEn: I received a letter from Japan.
examples.romaji: Nihon kara no tegami wo uketorimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は東京からの旅行者です。
examples.translation: He is a traveler from Tokyo.
examples.translationEn: He is a traveler from Tokyo.
examples.romaji: Kare wa Tokyo kara no ryokousha desu.

examples.sentence: 昨日、母からの電話がありました。
examples.translation: Yesterday, I got a call from my mother.
examples.translationEn: Yesterday, I got a call from my mother.
examples.romaji: Kinou, haha kara no denwa ga arimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は大学からの友人です。
examples.translation: She is a friend from college.
examples.translationEn: She is a friend from college.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa daigaku kara no yuujin desu.
